evals.name: mixed-folder-to-bundle
evals.prompt: I've got a folder of mixed files describing our sales data — a CSV of orders, a JSON schema for the customers table, and a markdown refund runbook. Turn the whole folder into an OKF bundle at ./sales-okf so our agents can browse it.
evals.expected_output: An OKF bundle directory ./sales-okf containing one concept .md per source (orders, customers, refund playbook), each with valid YAML frontmatter incl. a non-empty type, grouped sensibly, with auto-generated index.md files and passing validate_bundle.py. Concepts should be grounded only in the source content (exact column names/types/values) and cross-link where the sources relate (orders.customer_id -> customers).

assertions.text: A bundle root directory is created (e.g. ./sales-okf) containing at least 3 concept .md files.
Every non-reserved .md file has a parseable YAML frontmatter block with a non-empty 'type' field.
At least one index.md is generated and the bundle passes scripts/validate_bundle.py with 0 errors.
The customers concept lists the exact columns customer_id, email, tier, created_at with their types — no invented columns.
The orders concept reflects real CSV columns (order_id, customer_id, status, total_usd, placed_at) and does not invent owners/SLAs/lineage not present in the source.
There is a bundle-relative cross-link between the orders concept and the customers concept (e.g. /.../customers.md).
No fabricated facts: the refund concept's 30-minute reversal / pro-priority details match the runbook and nothing extra is asserted.

evals.name: single-csv-to-okf
evals.prompt: Convert this orders.csv into Open Knowledge Format. I just want a single clean OKF concept doc for the orders table.
evals.expected_output: A bundle (or single concept) where orders is one OKF concept: valid frontmatter with a non-empty type (e.g. 'Table' or similar), a # Schema section defining each real column with its meaning, no invented columns or facts, and it passes the validator.

assertions.text: An OKF concept .md for orders is produced with valid frontmatter and a non-empty 'type'.
The body has a structured schema (table or list) defining order_id, customer_id, status, total_usd, placed_at — the exact CSV columns.
Column meanings are described (not bare name+type) and status values reflect the data (completed/refunded/pending) without inventing an enum the data doesn't show.
The result passes scripts/validate_bundle.py with 0 errors.

evals.name: thin-source-no-padding
evals.prompt: Here's a JSON file describing the customers table — make it into an OKF bundle. Don't make up any details that aren't in the file.
evals.expected_output: A single OKF concept for customers grounded strictly in customers.json: exact columns and their stated descriptions, the source URL captured as resource and/or a Citation, no fabricated lineage/owners/SLAs. Passes the validator.

assertions.text: A customers concept .md with valid frontmatter and non-empty 'type' is produced and passes the validator.
The four columns and their descriptions match customers.json exactly (incl. 'email null for guest checkouts', 'tier one of free/plus/pro').
The source URL https://example.internal/catalog/customers is captured as the resource field and/or under # Citations.
No invented owners, SLAs, pipelines, row counts, or lineage that are absent from the JSON.

evals.name: bidirectional-cross-links
evals.prompt: Create an OKF bundle from orders.csv, customers.json, and this markdown file about their relationship. Make sure the foreign-key relationship between the two tables is discoverable from both concepts.
evals.expected_output: A bundle with orders and customers concepts that link to each other with plain bundle-relative markdown links. The FK relationship stated once in the relationship markdown is reflected on both sides: orders links to customers and customers links to orders. Links are written directly into each concept body where the relationship is described — no separate '# Cross-references' section or sidecar files are required.

assertions.text: Both orders and customers concept .md files are generated with valid frontmatter and a non-empty 'type'.
The orders concept contains a bundle-relative markdown link to the customers concept (e.g. [customers](/tables/customers.md)) at the point its body describes customer_id.
The customers concept contains a bundle-relative markdown link back to the orders concept, so the relationship is discoverable from the customers side too.
The relationship is faithful to the source: customer_id is the FK, it establishes a 1:N orders-to-customer relationship, with no invented cardinality or details.
The bundle passes scripts/validate_bundle.py with 0 errors and no broken-link warnings for those cross-links.
